summary.benchmark_name: BERT-base style 12-layer encoder model - encoder_block - Attention scores
summary.description: Representative transformer layer spec 'encoder_block' used 12 time(s) in full-model aggregation. Dense BERT-base style full encoder model summary with 12 identical encoder layers. This is a transformer-model workflow example, not a published accelerator calibration card. Generated decomposed card for Attention scores. Formula: B * heads * S * S * head_dim. Matmul shape is 128 x 64 times 64 x 128; operation multiplicity is 12. The right operand is activation data for attention, so cross-batch weight-stationary reuse is disabled in this generated card.

Source-backed card for Feldmann et al. Nature 2021. The published reference is an integrated photonic tensor core for convolutional processing; this config uses a small dense matmul tile as a PhotonicBench surrogate and does not claim to reproduce the paper dataflow.

The right operand is activation data for attention, so cross-batch weight-stationary reuse is disabled in this generated card.

Source-backed card for the Science Advances 2025 HITOP optical tensor processor. The published system uses space-time-wavelength hypermultiplexing; this config uses a 64x64 dense local matmul surrogate for PhotonicBench comparison only.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported HITOP efficiency/scale claims; matmul-surrogate local model

summary.benchmark_name: Lin 2024 TFLN 120 GOPS tensor core surrogate
summary.description: Source-backed card for the Nature Communications 2024 thin-film lithium niobate photonic tensor core. The published device performs large fan-in neural-network layers; this config uses a 16x16 dense local matmul surrogate for comparison only.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported speed/training claims; matmul-surrogate local model

summary.description: Audit-sized 64x64 photonic matmul example. This is not yet a calibrated reproduction of a published accelerator result.

summary.benchmark_name: Meng 2025 MRR OTPU tensor core surrogate
summary.description: Source-backed card for the Light: Science & Applications 2025 high-integrated microring-resonator optical tensor processing unit. The published result is a multidomain tensor-convolution OTPU; this config uses a 16x16 dense local matmul surrogate for comparison only.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported computing-density/accuracy claims; matmul-surrogate local model

summary.description: Source-backed calibration card for the Nature 2025 PACE 64x64 photonic matrix-vector accelerator. The component model remains a simple local assumption set; the published calibration table carries the paper-reported values.

Source-backed calibration card for the Nature 2025 PACE 64x64 photonic matrix-vector accelerator. The component model remains a simple local assumption set; the published calibration table carries the paper-reported values.

Source-backed card for the APL Photonics 2025 AWGR-based multidimensional photonic AI accelerator. The local workload is a 16x16 dense tile surrogate for the reported 16x16 AWGR tensor engine, not an exact Matrix-by-Tensor-Multiply reproduction.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported and paper-projected throughput/energy targets; matmul-surrogate local model

summary.description: Source-backed card for the Science 2024 Taichi photonic chiplet. The published reference is a diffractive-interference hybrid optical neural-network chiplet; this config uses a 64x64 dense local matmul surrogate because PhotonicBench does not model Taichi's distributed optical protocol.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported energy/scale targets; matmul-surrogate local model

summary.benchmark_name: Small transformer sanity layer - Attention scores
summary.description: Tiny dense transformer layer shape for exact MAC-count sanity checks. This is a synthetic helper example, not a published accelerator calibration card. Generated decomposed card for Attention scores. Formula: B * heads * S * S * head_dim. Matmul shape is 4 x 4 times 4 x 4; operation multiplicity is 4. The right operand is activation data for attention, so cross-batch weight-stationary reuse is disabled in this generated card.

The right operand is activation data for attention, so cross-batch weight-stationary reuse is disabled in this generated card.

Synthetic component-model example that exercises operand reuse, weight-stationary execution, pipelining, and separate vector/weight DAC assumptions. This is not a published accelerator calibration card.

Source-backed benchmark card for Xu et al. Nature 2021. The published reference is an optical vector convolution accelerator; this config uses PhotonicBench's matmul path as a dense vector-by-kernel surrogate only, not as an exact convolution dataflow reproduction.
summary.provenance_status: paper-reported throughput/workload targets; matmul-surrogate local model

comparison_presets.name: Published reference surrogate cards
comparison_presets.description: Source-backed cards with paper metrics kept as published references and local workloads labeled as surrogates.

comparison_presets.name: Local reuse sensitivity
comparison_presets.description: Compare the simple local 64x64 card against the batch weight-stationary variant.

modeling_boundaries: Local model estimates come from local_model fields.
Published references remain separate under published_reference.
Transformer aggregate timing is serial, not a fused scheduler claim.
Transformer model timing is a weighted serial summary over layer summaries, not a fused full-model scheduler claim.
Transformer aggregate noise is diagnostic and non-additive.
Transformer-layer exclusions are not modeled matmul costs.
Interface memory traffic is derived from converter bit widths and reuse counts, not a full memory hierarchy simulation.
System movement energy is a local SRAM/off-chip tier estimate added separately from core photonic compute/conversion energy.
